dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6850 IH: Deployment of Required Impairment Prevention Vehicle Equipment to Honor the Abbas Family Legacy to Terminate Drunk Driving Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Dingell introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend title 49, United States Code, to require manufacture, sale, or import of a minimum number of motor vehicles that met certain standards for drunk and impaired driving prevention, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Deployment of Required Impairment Prevention Vehicle Equipment to Honor the Abbas Family Legacy to Terminate Drunk Driving Act of 2025 or the DRIVE to HALT Drunk Driving Act.

subsection.text: Chapter 301 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by inserting after section 30129 the following:

Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this section, a covered manufacturer shall not manufacture for sale, sell, offer for sale, introduce or deliver for introduction in interstate commerce, or import into the United States less than—

paragraph.text: 10,000 passenger motor vehicles per calendar year that meet the performance specifications of the DADSS Subsystem Performance Specification Document; and
10,000 passenger motor vehicles per calendar year that comply with section 3.5.1 through 3.5.4.4 of the European New Car Assessment Programme version 10.3 (published December 2023).

Except as provided in paragraph (2), not later than 120 days after the European New Car Assessment Programme revises the standard described in subsection (a)(2) or a subsequent standard, such revised standards shall replace the standard described in subsection (a)(2).

The Secretary, after publishing a notice in the Federal Register and allowing for a period of public comment and review, may make a determination that the revised standard described in paragraph (1) does not meet the need for motor vehicle safety.

Should the Secretary make a determination that the revised standard described in paragraph (1) does not meet the need for motor vehicle safety pursuant to paragraph (2), the revised or new standards shall not replace the standard described in subsection (a)(2).

The term covered manufacturer means a manufacturer that, in the second most recent calendar year, manufactured for sale, sold, offered for sale, introduced or delivered for introduction in interstate commerce, or imported into the United States more than 250,000 motor vehicles.
The term passenger motor vehicle means a motor vehicle that is not a commercial motor vehicle (as defined in section 31101).

subsection.text: The requirements of this section shall expire upon the effective date of the rule required under section 24220 of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (49 U.S.C. 30111 note).
The analysis for subchapter II of chapter 301 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 30129 the following:
Section 30165(a)(1) of title 49, United States Code, is amended, in the first sentence, by inserting 30130(a), after 30127,.

subsection.header: Amendments to title 49, united states code
subsection.text: Section 30102(b)(1) of title 49, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (G), by striking and at the end;
in subparagraph (H), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: a minimum standard for motor vehicle or motor vehicle equipment performance; and
